newsSince: On June 23 Elkem's former Silicones division was rebranded Bluestar Silicones, following the April 30 close of the majority-stake divestiture to Bluestar. This is a cosmetic confirmation of the structural exit, not new substance, but it reinforces that the silicones leg of this page's thesis is now largely outside Elkem.

verdicts.rankRationale: Strict latent-node lens: the June 23 Bluestar rebrand confirms the silicones division (half this page's thermal/sealing/battery thesis) is now divested. Prior case already moved it down; rebrand is cosmetic but keeps hollowing the AI-pathway story. Small negative drift.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Residual carbon/specialty-materials exposure if thermal/battery demand spills beyond incumbents.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Silicones divestiture removes the core leg of the latent thermal/sealing AI-materials thesis.
verdicts.bull: Slimmed metals/materials/carbon focus could still serve thermal and battery-adjacent niches.
verdicts.neutral: Post-divestiture Elkem is a cyclical commodity-materials story with only tangential AI optionality.
verdicts.bear: The very silicones unit the page leaned on is gone; remaining business is low-margin cyclical metals/carbon.
